flights.intro: Compare prices to Buenos Aires and regional airports. Search is powered by Aviasales — booking and payment happen on the partner site.

flights.travelPrep.title: Tickets, transfers & connectivity
flights.travelPrep.flightsDesc: Compare flights to Argentina and between regions

flights.route.heroSubtitle: Indicative prices and calendar for {origin} — {destination}. Compare via Aviasales.
flights.route.defaultIntro: Compare flights on {origin} — {destination}: current price hints, monthly calendar, and full search on Aviasales.
flights.route.mow-bue.intro: Main route for tourists and relocators: direct and connecting flights from Moscow to Buenos Aires (EZE and AEP airports).
flights.route.mow-igr.intro: Flights to the Iguazú region — gateway to the falls. Often cheaper via Buenos Aires with a domestic leg.
flights.route.bue-brc.intro: Domestic flight from Buenos Aires to Bariloche — lakes and Patagonia mountains. Usually from AEP or EZE.
flights.route.bue-ush.intro: Ushuaia, the southernmost city. Domestic flight from Buenos Aires ~3.5 hours; book early in high season.
flights.route.bue-fte.intro: El Calafate — gateway to Perito Moreno glacier. Domestic from Buenos Aires; prices rise in peak season.
flights.route.bue-sla.intro: Salta and northwest Argentina. Domestic flight from Buenos Aires.
flights.route.bue-mdz.intro: Mendoza — wine capital at the Andes. Convenient domestic flight from Buenos Aires.
flights.route.bue-igr.intro: Domestic flight from Buenos Aires to Iguazú — the easiest way to reach the falls. Usually from AEP or EZE, about two hours in the air.
flights.route.relatedSubtitle: Other routes from the same city or to the same region.
flights.route.faqIntro: Answers about prices and booking. Data is indicative — verify before purchase.
flights.route.faq.price.a: Price depends on season, travel dates, and stops. This page shows Aviasales hints; full search gives exact fares.
flights.route.faq.booking.a: Argentina Travel does not sell tickets. Compare and book on partner Aviasales via buttons on this page.
flights.route.faq.domestic.q: Do I need a separate domestic ticket in Argentina?
flights.route.faq.domestic.a: Yes: international flight to Buenos Aires and domestic segments are separate tickets. Often fly to EZE/AEP first, then buy domestic.
flights.route.noTeaserBody: No cached price for this route. Open Aviasales search for all flights and current fares.

esim.title: Travel eSIM
esim.subtitle: Mobile data in Argentina and the region
esim.intro: Choose an Airalo eSIM package — activate before departure and skip the airport SIM queue. Purchase and setup happen on the partner site.
esim.howItWorks.title: How it works
esim.howItWorks.step1.title: Pick a package
esim.howItWorks.step1.description: Compare data volume, validity, and network operator for your destination.
esim.howItWorks.step2.title: Buy before you fly
esim.howItWorks.step2.description: Checkout on Airalo — the QR code arrives by email right after payment.
esim.howItWorks.step3.title: Activate on arrival
esim.howItWorks.step3.description: Install the eSIM profile in phone settings and enable data roaming.
esim.results.title: Available packages
esim.results.empty: No packages found for this country in the feed. Open Airalo for more plans and live prices.
esim.results.unconfigured: eSIM catalog is not configured — set AIRALO_FEED_URL or AIRALO_FEED_PATH (download the feed from your Travelpayouts dashboard). Continue on Airalo for now:
esim.offer.buyHint: Selected package on Airalo — confirm and pay
esim.results.noUnlimitedHint: The partner XML feed has no unlimited packages for this country. Airalo.com may list plans not yet in the feed — open “All packages on Airalo”.
esim.results.filteredEmpty: No packages match the selected filters.
esim.why.title: Why do more than 30 million people choose Airalo?
esim.why.item1: Local, regional, and international plans for 200+ destinations
esim.why.item2: Flexible packages, including unlimited data options
esim.why.item3: The app is available in 53 languages and supports multiple currencies
esim.why.item4: Easy setup — stay connected in just a few minutes
esim.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
esim.faq.q1: When should I install my eSIM?
esim.faq.a1: Install as soon as you'd like — just make sure you have a stable internet connection. You can install in advance if you're outside the coverage area; when you arrive, you can connect to use your package.
esim.faq.q2: How do I use my eSIM?
esim.faq.a2: When you're in the eSIM's coverage area, you can connect to a mobile network to use your eSIM. Make sure the eSIM is on and selected for data (or calls and texts, if included with your eSIM).
esim.faq.a3: In many cases, you can top up your eSIM — you don't need to buy a new eSIM to stay connected. When available, you'll find top-up packages for your eSIM before and after purchase.
esim.faq.a4: With renewals, you can automatically renew your package when your eSIM has 10% data remaining — turn renewals on and off from your eSIM's details.
esim.faq.support.title: Support
esim.faq.support.description: Need help? We offer 24/7, multi-language support.
esim.disclaimer: Prices and data allowances are indicative. Argentina Travel does not sell eSIMs — purchase and activation are completed on Airalo.
carRental.title: Car rental
carRental.page.title: Car rental
carRental.page.tagline: Find a car for Mendoza, Patagonia, and the northwest — compare classes and terms with partner LocalRent.
carRental.page.disclaimer: Search and booking are completed on the LocalRent partner site. Argentina Travel does not sell car rentals and may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.
carRental.subtitle: Rent a car for trips across Argentina
carRental.description: Find a car for routes outside major cities — Mendoza wineries, Patagonia roads, the northwest. Search and booking are handled by our partner.
carRental.intro: A car makes sense where public transport is sparse: between Patagonia towns, in the Mendoza mountains, and on Salta routes. In central Buenos Aires, metro, taxis, and transfers are usually enough — see our
carRental.partnerNote: Choose dates, pickup location, and car class. Payment and the rental agreement are completed on the LocalRent partner site.
carRental.benefits.title: Why rent through LocalRent
carRental.benefits.item1: Pickup at airports and cities — convenient right after arrival.
carRental.benefits.item2: Full insurance and clear terms — read the contract before paying.
carRental.benefits.item3: Multiple classes: from compacts to SUVs for gravel roads.
carRental.benefits.item4: Online booking without phone calls — confirmation by email.
carRental.regions.title: Where a car really helps
carRental.regions.intro: In these regions distances are long and buses do not always go where you want. More on transport in our
carRental.regions.mendoza.title: Mendoza and wineries
carRental.regions.mendoza.description: Roads between wineries and the Andes — a car gives route freedom without tour buses.
carRental.regions.patagonia.title: Patagonia
carRental.regions.patagonia.description: El Calafate, Bariloche, El Chaltén — hundreds of kilometres between towns. A car is needed for national parks and viewpoints.
carRental.regions.salta.title: Salta and the northwest
carRental.regions.salta.description: Canyons, salt flats, and high-altitude roads — rental works well for multi-day loop routes.
carRental.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
carRental.faq.q1: Do I need an international driving permit?
carRental.faq.a1: Yes. Rentals in Argentina usually require a valid national licence and an International Driving Permit (IDP). Confirm requirements with the rental company before booking.
carRental.faq.a2: We recommend full coverage (CDW/LDW) with zero or minimal excess. Basic policies often exclude windscreen, tyre, and underbody damage — important on Patagonia gravel roads.
carRental.faq.q3: Can I pick up in Buenos Aires and drop off elsewhere?
carRental.faq.a3: It depends on the supplier and route. Set different pickup and return locations in search — the widget will show if one-way rental is available. A fee often applies.
carRental.faq.q4: Should I rent a car in central Buenos Aires?
carRental.faq.a4: For city-only trips — usually no: heavy traffic, paid parking, and metro/taxis work better. A car makes sense if you are heading to the regions for several days.
carRental.disclaimer: Argentina Travel is not a car rental company and does not sell rentals. Search, booking, and payment are completed on the LocalRent partner site. Terms, prices, and availability are set by the partner.

insurance.title: Travel insurance
insurance.subtitle: Medical coverage for your trip to Argentina
insurance.description: Compare policies online: medical care, emergency help, and evacuation. Travel insurance is strongly recommended for entry — arrange it before departure.
insurance.partnerNote: Enter trip dates, age, and activities — pricing and payment happen on the insurance partner site via Travelpayouts.
insurance.checkoutHint: You came from tour checkout — get a policy for {count}.
insurance.disclaimer: Argentina Travel is not an insurer and does not sell policies. Terms, coverage, and claims are handled by the partner. Read the policy wording carefully before payment.
insurance.benefits.title: Why arrange insurance in advance
insurance.benefits.item1: Planned care and evacuation from remote Patagonia regions
insurance.benefits.item2: Peace of mind at the border — cover for the full stay
insurance.benefits.item3: Optional cover for active travel and trekking
insurance.benefits.item4: Online purchase in minutes — policy delivered by email
audioGuides.title: Audio guides in Argentina
audioGuides.subtitle: Explore the city at your own pace — no group, no schedule
audioGuides.description: Self-guided audio tours in the WeGoTrip app: professional narration, offline navigation, and museum tickets where needed.
audioGuides.partnerNote: Purchase and tour playback happen on WeGoTrip (Travelpayouts). We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.
audioGuides.empty: No audio guides for this city yet. Try search or another filter.
audioGuides.disclaimer: Argentina Travel does not sell audio tours — payment and playback are on WeGoTrip. You'll need headphones and the WeGoTrip app on your phone.
audioGuides.benefits.title: Why an audio guide works well on a trip
audioGuides.benefits.item1: Go at your own pace — no group or fixed schedule
audioGuides.benefits.item2: Route and navigation inside the WeGoTrip app
audioGuides.benefits.item3: Great for a first look at a neighborhood
audioGuides.benefits.item4: Start right after purchase — no waiting for a guide

tripPrep.title: Trip preparation
about.meta.title: About the project
about.meta.description: Go Argentina is a marketplace for curated tours in Argentina — mission, values, and how the platform works.
about.hero.title: A marketplace for curated tours with Argentine character
about.hero.subtitle: We connect travelers with trusted organizers — from Patagonian glaciers to tango in Buenos Aires. Clear booking, honest feedback, no fake ratings.
about.story.title: Who we are and why
about.story.p1: Go Argentina is a Russian-language platform for booking curated tours in Argentina. We are not a tour operator: each route is run by an independent organizer with their own program and pricing.
about.story.p2: Our goal is to make choosing a tour simple and safe: clear listings, filters by region and activity, and a personal account for requests and messages.
about.value.travelers.title: For travelers
about.value.travelers.text: Curated routes with transparent terms, real reviews, and no prepayment until the organizer confirms.
about.value.organizers.title: For organizers
about.value.organizers.text: Tools to publish tours, manage requests, and chat with guests — without heavy bureaucracy at the start.
about.value.trust.title: Trust and honesty
about.value.trust.text: We do not invent stats: ratings and reviews appear after real trips. New tours are marked as “New”.
about.explore.title: More on the platform
about.explore.subtitle: Beyond the tour catalog — places directory, travel guide, and immigration resources for relocation planning.
